question: what's the new macos called?
resolution: agentic panel converged; gold overwritten 'macOS Golden Gate' -> 'macOS 27 Golden Gate' | AUDIT: verbose overwrite 'macOS 27 Golden Gate' judged SAME fact as original 'macOS Golden Gate' (merely wordier); reverted gold to crisp 'macOS Golden Gate', verbatim preserved.

openrouter:anthropic/claude-sonnet-5.answer:
The new macOS announced at WWDC 2026 is called **macOS 27 "Golden Gate"** — named after San Francisco's Golden Gate Bridge. It's the successor to macOS 26 "Tahoe" and is set to launch in fall 2026. Notably, it's the first version of macOS to run exclusively on Apple silicon Macs, while also being the last version with full Rosetta 2 support.

Sources: [Mashable](https://mashable.com/tech/macos-27-
